title: AST-Aware Codemods
concept: Using syntax tree parsers to apply precise, structural rewrites instead of risky regex find-and-replace.

exercises.prompt: What is a good reason for consolidating duplicates in code?

options.text: It makes the program longer and harder to read.
It helps to avoid repetition and makes the code cleaner.
It eliminates all programming errors forever.

exercises.explain: Consolidating duplicates helps to keep your code clean and easier to maintain.

cards.title: AST-Aware Codemods
cards.body: AST stands for Abstract Syntax Tree. It helps us understand the structure of code so we can change it safely.

cards.title: What is Codemod?
cards.body: A codemod is a tool that makes large-scale code transformations in a safer way by understanding code structure.

cards.title: Looking Inside a Book
cards.body: Using AST is like reading a book with the pages opened. You can see the whole story and change sentences easily, unlike flipping through a stack of pages.

cards.title: Example of AST Codemod
cards.body: Imagine you need to change every function call in your code. With an AST, you can target the function specifically and change it without touching anything else.

cards.title: Myth: Regex is Always Enough
cards.body: Many believe regex can do it all. However, regex can break complex code. AST handles complex structures better!

cards.title: AST vs Regex
cards.body: Using AST is like using a blueprint; you can see the whole structure before making changes.

exercises.prompt: What does AST help us do better than regex?

options.text: Make precise structural changes without breaking code.
Increase the length of the code unnecessarily.

exercises.explain: AST helps us make precise changes to the code structure, which is much safer than using regex.

exercises.prompt: AST makes code changes more risky than regex. True or False?
exercises.explain: False! AST makes changes safer by understanding code structure.

exercises.prompt: An AST stands for Abstract _____ Tree.
exercises.sentence: An AST stands for Abstract Syntax Tree.
exercises.explain: Syntax is the correct term that refers to the structure of programming languages.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about ASTs is true?

options.text: ASTs help us change code without understanding its structure.
ASTs make it easy to apply precise code rewrites.
ASTs are just another name for regex tools.

exercises.explain: ASTs are specifically designed for understanding and transforming code, making them much more effective than regex.

exercises.prompt: Why should developers choose AST-based codemods over regex for code changes?

options.text: ASTs guarantee that codes will not change at all.
ASTs are unsafe but faster than regex changes.
ASTs allow developers to make precise, structural changes safely.
ASTs are cheaper to implement than regex.

exercises.explain: ASTs enable precise control over code structures, making it safer to implement changes than using regex, which often lacks the reliability.
